headline: Expat Salary Floor Doubles to RM20K from 1 June 2026; The Pipeline Question Remains
context: From 1 June 2026, the Employment Pass Category I minimum rises from RM10,000 to RM20,000. Category III is capped at 5 years with mandatory replacement plans. The policy aims to focus expatriate hiring on high-impact expertise.

cards.big: Malaysia doubles the Category I Employment Pass minimum salary to RM20,000 from 1 June 2026.
cards.sub: Official framing: focus expatriate hiring on high-impact expertise; Category III replacement-plan requirements include training, mentoring and documentation.

cards.big: Semiconductor target: 60,000 workers by 2030. Government data cites about 6,600 E&E-related bachelor's-degree graduates per year.
cards.sub: World Bank documents Malaysian brain drain, with Singapore as the main destination. The price-of-replacements lever is separate from the pipeline.

cards.big: The new floor scales differently across firm sizes. DOSM: MSMEs are 39.5% of GDP and 48.7% of employment in 2024.
cards.sub: Category I foreign hires now face double the threshold. The official FAQ says monitoring will use documentation and periodic reports.

cards.big: Singapore's EP minimum qualifying salary is SGD5,600 (~RM18,500) for most sectors, SGD6,200 (~RM20,500) for financial services.
cards.sub: Malaysia's RM20K sits above the non-finance Singapore floor. Comparative competitiveness depends on the full cost package.

cards.big: The salary floor adjusts the price of foreign replacement. The pipeline question (graduates, retention, brain-drain destinations) is a separate set of policies.
Protecting Malaysian jobs is a legitimate aim. The risk is that protection without parallel pipeline investment becomes paper compliance rather than specialist development.
